symptoms.symptom_name: Syncope triggered by acute emotional stress with rapid full recovery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Emotion/exertion-triggered syncope is a defining clinical feature of CPVT.
Adrenergically-triggered syncope is also characteristic of long QT syndrome.
Emotional triggers also cause benign reflex syncope — cannot alone distinguish benign from malignant cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid forceful palpitations immediately preceding syncope

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tachyarrhythmic prodrome indicates the syncope is arrhythmic rather than reflex-mediated.
Classic vasovagal syncope is usually preceded by nausea, warmth and greying-out, not sudden fast palpitations.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent exertional presyncope (dizziness during hard sport) over the past year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional presyncope reflects the same catecholamine-driven arrhythmic mechanism and shows the event was not isolated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy / structural cause of exertional syncope
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional presyncope is a classic warning symptom of structural exertional cardiac disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of juvenile sudden cardiac death during exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Juvenile sudden death in the family markedly raises the probability of a heritable arrhythmia syndrome.
Premature sudden-death family history is a recognised risk marker for inherited long QT syndrome.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy / structural cause of exertional syncope
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HCM is a leading heritable cause of sudden death in young athletes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid return to baseline, no post-ictal confusion, no convulsive features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immediate full recovery without a post-ictal state argues strongly against a generalised seizure and points to a cardiac (arrhythmic) cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain or shortness of breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute coronary / pulmonary cause of syncope
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain or dyspnoea makes an ischaemic or pulmonary embolic cause less likely, though does not exclude an arrhythmic mechanism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal resting cardiovascular examination (dual heart sounds, no murmur, regular rhythm, symmetric pulses)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal resting exam is EXPECTED in CPVT and does not exclude it; sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Typically normal in CPVT; primarily needed to exclude long QT syndrome and other repolarisation/conduction abnormalities
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Long QT syndrome and other ECG-detectable causes are missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A resting ECG measuring the QT interval is essential to exclude long QT syndrome — impossible via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bidirectional / polymorphic ventricular tachycardia on exercise (graded stress) test

investigation_details.investigation_name: Exercise / graded stress test with continuous ECG
investigation_details.expected_result: Reproducible bidirectional or polymorphic ventricular tachycardia emerging with increasing adrenergic load
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnostic hallmark of CPVT is never demonstrated and the condition is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional bidirectional VT in a structurally normal heart with a normal resting ECG is the defining finding of CPVT — a specialist provocative test, never available via telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Structurally normal heart (excludes hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and other structural causes of exertional syncope)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A structural cause such as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy is not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy / structural cause of exertional syncope
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiography is needed to exclude a structural cause of exertional syncope and sudden death.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Adrenergically-triggered syncope with a palpitation prodrome, recurrent exertional presyncope, and juvenile sudden-death family history together define high-risk syncope demanding urgent cardiac evaluation and activity restriction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line same-day test to exclude long QT syndrome and screen for other conduction/repolarisation abnormalities; must be obtained in person.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent cardiology / electrophysiology referral with exercise stress test and ambulatory monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Provocative testing is required to demonstrate the CPVT-defining arrhythmia and to risk-stratify; the AI Doctor should facilitate this pathway, not attempt it.
Excludes hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and other structural causes of exertional syncope.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine cranial CT / EEG for presumed seizure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The presentation is a cardiac (arrhythmic) syncope, not a seizure — defaulting to neurological imaging/EEG would misdirect the workup and delay urgent cardiac assessment.
